mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month deceleration in the consumer price index reduces inflation risk premiums embedded in equity discount rates, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between CPIAUCSL changes and 20-day S&P 500 returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0%, it signals deteriorating household income expectations that reduce aggregate demand and compress corporate earnings forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and the consumer price index create a stagflationary financial tightening that compresses real earnings and raises discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint z-scores of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fail to predict negative 20-day returns with Rank IC greater than 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During consumer-led expansions, declines in retail sales signal imminent earnings downgrades for the discretionary sector that spill over into broad negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS changes coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations during expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production indicate immediate supply-side disruptions that raise unit costs and reduce operational leverage, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO contracts by more than 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls reveal coordinated weakness in construction and labor income channels, triggering a broad-based demand shock that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint negative changes in HOUST and PAYEMS fail to predict negative 60-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC greater than 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained reduction in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines do not outperform baseline with Rank IC greater than 0.04 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly contractions in real GDP signal aggregate demand shortfalls that reduce top-line revenue growth across the corporate sector, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 contracts.
